footnotes.text: The picture of the first beast is based on the seventh chapter of Daniel. This beast is the figure of the kingdoms of the world, kingdoms founded on passion and selfishness, which in every age are antagonistic to Christ and seek to oppress the servants of God. Imperial Rome represents this power.
The weapons of the saints are faith and patience; they must accept sufferings and persecution as Christ did His cross.

Mark: as slaves received a brand or a mark in their flesh, indicating to whom they belonged, so in the spiritual conflict there is on the side of good and of evil a brand or mark. St. Paul spoke of such marks in his own body that proved him a slave of Jesus Christ (Gal. 6, 17). So the false prophet seeks to impress a mark on all. Just what this mark is we do not know.

Six hundred and sixty-six: the most probable interpretation of the number is that it represents the name Caesar Neron, which in Hebrew characters make up the number 666. It symbolizes extreme imperfection, for each digit is one short of seven, the number that signifies perfection.
